location: Dalston Bank, Greenbrae Loaning, Dumfries, DG1 3DD
why_them: Website is significantly outdated — basic HTML/CSS, jQuery-era navigation menus, no responsive mobile layout, and a personal email address (rjmfinancial@me.com) as the primary contact, which undermines trust. No online appointment booking, no lead-capture form, and no modern CTA. A 20+ year IFA practice with this level of digital neglect is a prime rebuild candidate.
recommended_service: Full website redesign with modern responsive build, professional email setup, appointment booking integration, and client-facing financial tools

why_them: 40+ year established IFA firm built on outdated Concrete5 CMS with a fixed-width layout, no social media, no appointment booking, and RSS-aggregated news instead of original content. The site communicates heritage but fails on modern trust signals — no visible team photos, no SSL badge, no online client portal that works. Multi-adviser partnership suggests budget to invest.
recommended_service: Full CMS migration to modern platform, responsive redesign, client portal integration, and SEO content strategy

why_them: SSL certificate has expired — a fatal trust signal failure for an FCA-regulated financial adviser. Site is completely inaccessible over HTTPS, meaning browsers display a security warning before users even see the page. Newton Stewart IFA with 26 years experience and no functioning website is an urgent prospect.
recommended_service: Emergency website rebuild with SSL, modern responsive design, online enquiry form, and FCA compliance page

why_them: Mid-2010s Divi WordPress theme that hasn't been meaningfully updated, with legacy Facebook pixel code and construction-mode overlays still present. No online appointment booking, no mortgage calculator, and minimal content for SEO. A Castle Douglas-based sole practitioner broker covering all of D&G needs a more credible digital front door.
recommended_service: WordPress refresh or rebuild, mortgage calculator tool, Calendly booking integration, and local SEO optimisation

location: Offices F1-F4, Monreith House, The Crichton, Bankend Road, Dumfries, DG1 4ZZ
why_them: Multi-adviser firm at The Crichton business campus with broken images (blank.png placeholders) still live on the homepage — a critical trust failure for a financial planning firm. Contact details buried behind navigation, no chatbot or live enquiry, and a visually dated layout. Their client base across D&G, Cumbria, and beyond justifies investment in a professional web presence.
recommended_service: Site redesign with fixed image assets, prominent contact integration, online fact-find form, and blog for financial content marketing

why_them: FCA-authorised mortgage adviser and financial planner in Newton Stewart with no dedicated website — discoverable only through Unbiased and Yell directory listings. With 15+ years of experience and a whole-of-market offering, the absence of any web presence is a significant lead-generation gap that a simple professional site would immediately remedy.
recommended_service: New website build — clean, professional, with services overview, FCA credentials, and enquiry form

why_them: One of South West Scotland's most established insurance brokers (est. 1979, FCA ref 305903, 200+ products from 35+ insurers) operating on a 960px fixed-width website — completely non-responsive on mobile. The site uses mid-2000s jQuery and inline CSS throughout, with no social media presence and no online quote request tool. A long-standing firm of this size has both the need and the means to upgrade.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with mobile-responsive design, online enquiry/quote request form, and basic SEO

why_them: Modern-looking site but with serious credibility issues: business hours listed as 00:00-23:59 (clearly misconfigured), no team profiles, no visible email address, and no client testimonials. For a regulated insurance broker, these gaps actively damage trust. Site needs professional content strategy and credibility-building fixes more than a technical rebuild.
recommended_service: Website content overhaul — team pages, corrected business info, testimonials, and email visibility; possible UX improvements

location: 1 Kindar Road, New Abbey, Dumfries & Galloway, DG2 8DP
why_them: Local D&G IT firm trading since 2007 — website timed out on fetch, indicating severe performance issues or hosting problems, which is deeply ironic for an IT support company. Their own poor digital performance (slow/unreachable site) is a compelling conversation opener. Facebook presence but no visible modern site to back it up.
recommended_service: New fast-loading website with clear SME IT service packages, case studies, and a support ticket/contact system

business_name: Hazel J L Smith Chartered Architect
why_them: RIBA/RIAS chartered architect with BUILD award wins (2017, 2019) operating a website with an SSL/TLS error — browsers present a security warning before visitors see anything. Site also uses HTTP not HTTPS by default. An award-winning sole-practitioner architect with no functioning secure website is losing enquiries to the warning page alone.
recommended_service: New secure website with SSL, portfolio gallery, services page, planning/conservation credentials highlighted, and contact form
source: Google search / Build Magazine / direct site check

why_them: Central Dumfries architect on a Wix site that is heavily JavaScript-dependent with no visible phone number, email, or project portfolio accessible without JS rendering. On slow connections or with JS disabled, visitors see nothing useful. Operating since 2004 with no clear digital identity beyond an empty-feeling Wix shell.
recommended_service: Rebuild on a more SEO-friendly platform (WordPress) with a proper project gallery, team page, services breakdown, and clear contact details

why_them: Established Castle Douglas RIAS/RIBA practice (est. 1983, RIBA award winners) whose website is in a transitional state following the death of founder Simon Winstanley — a holding page notes 'new website under construction' with almost no services or portfolio content. The practice is likely continuing under new management and urgently needs a proper digital presence to retain its reputation and attract new clients.
recommended_service: New website build — portfolio showcase, services page, team/new leadership introduction, and enquiry form; brand refresh optional
source: ukpostcodecheck.com / RIAS directory / simonwinstanley.com direct assessment

why_them: Dumfries-based veteran-owned IT, gaming PC, and security services company with a single-page web presence that is visually contemporary but content-thin — no portfolio, no team info, no case studies, and no social media links. For a company selling trust-based IT support and SIA-led security services to local businesses, the absence of social proof and service depth is a conversion killer.
recommended_service: Multi-page website with dedicated IT support, gaming PC, and security services pages; portfolio/case studies; and Google Business optimisation

why_them: 30+ year independent broker with a modern-looking site, but no online quote engine despite having four distinct product lines (business, personal, farm, motor). The absence of self-service quoting in a market where comparison sites dominate is a clear competitive disadvantage. Farm Solutions specialism serving rural D&G is a strong niche that could be amplified digitally.
recommended_service: Online quote request tool for farm/rural insurance, landing page optimisation for agricultural niche, and content marketing around rural risk
